Documentation.Summary: Adds a search bar in the context menu. Useful for big menus

Identical to Menu except DeleteOnClose defaults to true.
Can optionally be made searchable by setting <see cref="P:Editor.ContextMenu.Searchable" /> to true before opening.
Examples: var menu = new ContextMenu { Searchable = true }; menu.AddOption( "Option 1", action: () => {} ); menu.OpenAtCursor();

Documentation.Summary: Register a named menubar target. This allows [Menu] to target a specific menubar.
Called when this option was clicked..
Called when this option was toggled.
Sets an icon for the option via a raw image.

Text to display if <see cref="P:Editor.Option.Text" /> is empty.
Whether this option is a toggle option. <see cref="P:Editor.Option.Checked" />.
Whether this option is toggled/checked. <see cref="P:Editor.Option.Checkable" />.
Whether this option can be clicked. Will also be visually different.

ConstructorArguments: Please use ShortcutName, which takes a shortcut ident (such as editor.save) instead of keys (such as CTRL+S).
